text.title: Product Development Trends 2026 Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Product Development Trends 2026 methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Every source MUST have a retrieval date within 18 months of the trend brief publish date.
2026 trend snapshot MUST cover these 6 buckets even if some receive 'ignore': on-device LLM, agent-as-product, AI-native CRM, privacy-first/DSA, vibe-coding, sustainability badges.
rule.rationale: Skipping a bucket hides a known trend; explicit ignore is better than silence.

rule.statement: Brief MUST include a one-table retrospective of 2025 bets and their 2026 verdict (still bet / now monitor / now ignore).
rule.rationale: Without retro, 2026 brief floats free of prior commitments.

rule.statement: Same as parent: every bet MUST carry a kill criterion phrased in 2026-observable terms.
rule.rationale: Bets without exit criteria become sunk-cost projects.

rule.statement: Same as parent: >=3 source classes (academic/filing, practitioner, market signal) per trend.
Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec — decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
